title: celtis rigescens
intro: Celtis rigescens, also known as the netleaf hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Celtis rigescens include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a digestive aid. Celtis rigescens can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
